info.title: CodeQualityToGitlab
info.description: Convert Dotnet warnings into Gitlab code quality format

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: Parser.Parse, OK: ParserExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParseResultExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResultExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Tool to convert Dotnet-Formats to Gitlab code quality

commands.name: roslynator
commands.description: Convert Roslynator file to Code Quality issue

arguments.name: root
arguments.description: The name root of the repository. Gitlab requires Code Quality issues to contain paths relative to the repository, but the tools report them as absolute file paths. Everything given in with this option will be removed. E.g. root is 'c:/dev' and the file name is something like 'c:/dev/myrepo/file.cs' it will transformed to 'myrepo/file.cs'. Can often be omitted.  [default: <path>]

commands.name: sarif
commands.description: Convert Sarif files to Code Quality issue

arguments.name: root
arguments.description: The name root of the repository. Gitlab requires Code Quality issues to contain paths relative to the repository, but the tools report them as absolute file paths. Everything given in with this option will be removed. E.g. root is 'c:/dev' and the file name is something like 'c:/dev/myrepo/file.cs' it will transformed to 'myrepo/file.cs'. Can often be omitted.  [default: <path>]

commands.name: merge
commands.description: Merge multiple code quality files into one

commands.name: transform
commands.description: Transforms files from a glob mapping and merges them to one file

arguments.name: sarifGlob
arguments.description: Glob pattern for the sarif files [default: **/*.sarif.json]

arguments.name: roslynatorGlob
arguments.description: Glob pattern for the roslynator files [default: **/roslynator.xml]

arguments.name: root
arguments.description: The name root of the repository. Gitlab requires Code Quality issues to contain paths relative to the repository, but the tools report them as absolute file paths. Everything given in with this option will be removed. E.g. root is 'c:/dev' and the file name is something like 'c:/dev/myrepo/file.cs' it will transformed to 'myrepo/file.cs'. Can often be omitted.  [default: <path>]
